data.title: US Ownership Shift Puts MP Materials At Center Of Rare Earth Supply
data.description:
• The U.S. government is shifting toward direct equity stakes in critical mineral companies, including a significant stake in MP Materials (NYSE:MP).
  • Recent funding and ownership moves involving USA Rare Earth Inc. and MP Materials highlight an effort to secure domestic rare earth supply for national security.
  • This policy direction positions MP Materials as a key beneficiary as the only significant rare earth miner in North America.

MP Materials operates in the rare earths sector, supplying materials that feed into defense, clean energy, and advanced electronics supply chains. With the Department of Defense now a major shareholder, MP is more closely aligned with U.S. policy efforts to build resilient, local critical mineral capacity.

For investors, this tighter link between MP and federal policy could influence factors such as access to capital, long term project planning, and customer relationships. It also raises new questions around how government ownership might affect governance, risk, and potential funding for future stages of MP’s rare earth processing and downstream ambitions.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for MP Materials by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on MP Materials.

Why MP Materials could be great value

The shift toward direct U.S. government equity stakes in rare earth companies signals that Washington now treats these assets more like core defense infrastructure than just another commodity play. For MP Materials, which already counts the Department of Defense as an effective 15% shareholder and operates the only significant rare earth mine in North America, this reinforces its role as a central piece of U.S. supply chain planning rather than a purely commercial operator.

Advertisement

MP Materials narrative, sharpened by "American Sovereign Wealth Fund" backing

This news tends to support the existing narrative that MP is tightly linked to U.S. national security priorities, with the Pentagon previously becoming its largest shareholder through a US$400 million share purchase. Government attention to USA Rare Earth and other peers does not remove MP from the story; it broadens the theme of a domestically anchored, government backed rare earth ecosystem where MP sits as an early and established exposure.

Risks and rewards that stand out for MP Materials

  • Policy support for rare earths, including large government equity stakes in MP and peers, signals sustained interest in building domestic supply chains tied to defense and clean energy.
  • MP has been highlighted as trading at a discount to some analyst fair value estimates and as having earnings growth forecasts, which some investors may view as potential upside if execution and policy support align.
  • Government ownership can come with conditions on capital allocation, pricing or exports, which may limit management flexibility compared with a fully private sector backed miner.
  • There is one flagged risk around insider selling, reminding investors to watch management share activity alongside policy headlines.

What to watch next for MP Materials

From here, it is worth watching how government funding is split between MP, USA Rare Earth and other projects, and whether MP secures additional contracts, processing support or downstream partnerships off the back of this policy shift. You can stay on top of how different investors interpret this by following the latest community views in this narrative hub, especially as new policy announcements and quarterly results come through.

data.title: Samsung AI Promoter Debut Raises Questions On Valuation And Growth Potential
data.description:
• Samsung Electronics and ESTsoft introduced the AI Promoter at CES 2026, an AI powered interactive solution for exhibitions.
  • The system supports real time multilingual conversations and connects with Samsung's Spatial Signage to create immersive visitor experiences.

For investors watching Samsung Electronics KOSE:A005930, this launch arrives with the share price at ₩152,100. The stock is up 1.9% over the past week and 30.0% over the past month, and has delivered triple digit returns over 1, 3 and 5 years. That backdrop frames the AI Promoter as part of a broader push into applied AI and display technology rather than a one off showcase.

The AI Promoter also highlights how Samsung is using its display hardware, software partnerships and AI capabilities together to deepen its role at large global events. For you as an investor, the focus is less on a single product and more on what this type of deployment could signal for exhibition, retail and public space solutions tied to Samsung platforms.

data.title: Should Weakness in Qingci Games Inc.'s (HKG:6633) Stock Be Seen As A Sign That Market Will Correct The Share Price Given Decent Financials?
data.description:
With its stock down 13% over the past week, it is easy to disregard Qingci Games (HKG:6633). But if you pay close attention, you might find that its key financial indicators look quite decent, which could mean that the stock could potentially rise in the long-term given how markets usually reward more resilient long-term fundamentals. Particularly, we will be paying attention to Qingci Games' ROE today.

Return on Equity or ROE is a test of how effectively a company is growing its value and managing investors’ money. In other words, it is a profitability ratio which measures the rate of return on the capital provided by the company's shareholders.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

How Do You Calculate Return On Equity?

Return on equity can be calculated by using the formula:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for Qingci Games is:

4.4% = CN¥82m ÷ CN¥1.9b (Based on the trailing twelve months to June 2025).

The 'return' is the profit over the last twelve months. That means that for every HK$1 worth of shareholders' equity, the company generated HK$0.04 in profit.

See our latest analysis for Qingci Games

What Has ROE Got To Do With Earnings Growth?

We have already established that ROE serves as an efficient profit-generating gauge for a company's future earnings. Depending on how much of these profits the company reinvests or "retains", and how effectively it does so, we are then able to assess a company’s earnings growth potential. Generally speaking, other things being equal, firms with a high return on equity and profit retention, have a higher growth rate than firms that don’t share these attributes.

Qingci Games' Earnings Growth And 4.4% ROE

As you can see, Qingci Games' ROE looks pretty weak. Even compared to the average industry ROE of 9.2%, the company's ROE is quite dismal. However, the moderate 19% net income growth seen by Qingci Games over the past five years is definitely a positive. We reckon that there could be other factors at play here. Such as - high earnings retention or an efficient management in place.

Next, on comparing with the industry net income growth, we found that Qingci Games' reported growth was lower than the industry growth of 25% over the last few years, which is not something we like to see.

Earnings growth is a huge factor in stock valuation. The investor should try to establish if the expected growth or decline in earnings, whichever the case may be, is priced in. This then helps them determine if the stock is placed for a bright or bleak future. If you're wondering about Qingci Games''s valuation, check out this gauge of its price-to-earnings ratio, as compared to its industry.

Is Qingci Games Efficiently Re-investing Its Profits?

Qingci Games doesn't pay any regular dividends, meaning that all of its profits are being reinvested in the business, which explains the fair bit of earnings growth the company has seen.

Conclusion

On the whole, we do feel that Qingci Games has some positive attributes. Namely, its respectable earnings growth, which it achieved due to it retaining most of its profits. However, given the low ROE, investors may not be benefitting from all that reinvestment after all. While we won't completely dismiss the company, what we would do, is try to ascertain how risky the business is to make a more informed decision around the company. To know the 2 risks we have identified for Qingci Games visit our risks dashboard for free.

data.title: Is Abercrombie & Fitch’s (ANF) NFL Fashion Deal the Next Step in Its Brand Reinvention?
data.description:
• Abercrombie & Fitch recently became the first official fashion partner of the NFL and hosted an invite-only, star-studded pre-Super Bowl fashion show, underscoring the league’s push to link sports and style.
  • This collaboration coincides with Abercrombie & Fitch’s strong operating backdrop, including best-in-class margins and very large multi-year earnings growth, amplifying attention on its brand repositioning.
  • Next, we’ll examine how the NFL fashion partnership, alongside recent operating momentum, shapes Abercrombie & Fitch’s broader investment narrative.

These 12 companies survived and thrived after COVID and have the right ingredients to survive Trump's tariffs. Discover why before your portfolio feels the trade war pinch.

Advertisement

What Is Abercrombie & Fitch's Investment Narrative?

An investor in Abercrombie & Fitch today has to believe the brand’s repositioning can keep resonating with consumers while its current margin profile remains intact. The new NFL fashion partnership fits that story by putting Abercrombie in front of a broader, culturally plugged-in audience, but on its own it is unlikely to move the needle on near-term earnings guidance, which already calls for mid-single-digit sales growth and solid double-digit operating margins. Instead, the deal slightly strengthens the brand and marketing side of the thesis at a time when the share price has pulled back and the company is aggressively repurchasing stock. The bigger swing factors still look like maintaining pricing power, sustaining traffic without heavy discounting, and managing any earnings pressure flagged by forecasts.

However, the same brand heat that supports margins can fade faster than many investors expect. Despite retreating, Abercrombie & Fitch's shares might still be trading 39% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Thirteen Simply Wall St Community valuations span roughly US$90 to US$159 per share, reflecting very different assumptions about Abercrombie’s future. Set against recent share price volatility and forecasts for softer earnings, it is worth weighing several of these views before deciding how this evolving brand story might fit into your portfolio.

Explore 13 other fair value estimates on Abercrombie & Fitch - why the stock might be worth 7% less than the current price!

  • Our free Abercrombie & Fitch research report provides a comprehensive fundamental analysis summarized in a single visual - the Snowflake - making it easy to evaluate Abercrombie & Fitch's overall financial health at a glance.

Interested In Other Possibilities?

data.title: A Look At Bankinter (BME:BKT) Valuation After Strong Q4 Earnings And Solid Shareholder Returns
data.description:
Bankinter (BME:BKT) is in focus after reporting fourth quarter 2025 results, with net interest income of €576.38 million and net income of €278.47 million, both higher than the prior year.

See our latest analysis for Bankinter.

The earnings release comes after a strong period for investors, with a 1 day share price return of 2.72% and a 90 day share price return of 10.64% at a latest share price of €14.35. The 1 year total shareholder return of 80.96% and 5 year total shareholder return of roughly 4.5x suggest momentum has been building over both shorter and longer horizons.

If strong results from Bankinter have you thinking more broadly about financial opportunities, it could be a good time to see what else is working in the market with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With Bankinter trading close to its analyst price target and showing a roughly 30% implied discount to some intrinsic value estimates, the big question is whether there is still an entry point here or whether the market is already pricing in future growth.

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 2% Undervalued

At a last close of €14.35 versus a widely followed fair value estimate of about €14.65, the narrative points to a small valuation gap that rests on specific growth and margin assumptions.

Analysts have nudged their fair value estimate for Bankinter to about €14.65 from roughly €13.92, citing updated assumptions on revenue growth, profit margins and future P/E, in line with recent upward revisions to Street price targets.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what sits behind that higher fair value? The narrative leans on steadier top line expansion, firmer margins and a richer future earnings multiple. Want to see how those pieces fit together? Read the full narrative to unpack the assumptions in detail.

Result: Fair Value of €14.65 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, you still have to weigh the reliance on Spain and the possibility of tougher regulation; either factor could pressure margins and the current valuation narrative.

Find out about the key risks to this Bankinter narrative.

Another Way To Look At The Price

While our fair value estimate suggests Bankinter trades about 30.4% below its intrinsic value, the earnings multiple tells a tighter story. The shares trade on a P/E of 11.8x versus a fair ratio of 11.1x and peer averages of 11.5x in Spain and 11.1x across European banks. That small premium points to less obvious mispricing and more debate about how much quality you are willing to pay for.

data.title: Is Trade Desk (TTD) Now Offering A Long Term Opportunity After A 1 Year Slump
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Trade Desk's current share price lines up with what the business might be worth, you are not alone. This article will help you frame that question clearly.
  • The stock last closed at US$33.81, with returns of a 4.7% decline over 7 days, an 11.7% decline over 30 days, a 10.3% decline year to date, a 71.6% decline over 1 year, a 31.3% decline over 3 years, and a 57.4% decline over 5 years. That track record may change how you think about its potential risk and reward.
  • These moves sit against a backdrop of ongoing interest in Trade Desk's role in digital advertising and how investors see its position versus other ad tech players. Recent coverage has focused on where Trade Desk fits in a crowded market and how that context may be affecting sentiment around the stock.
  • On our checks, Trade Desk scores a 3 out of 6 valuation score, which means it screens as undervalued on half of the six methods assessed. Next, we will unpack those approaches before finishing with a way to look at value that goes beyond any single metric.

Find out why Trade Desk's -71.6% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Trade Desk Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back into today’s dollars. It is essentially a way of asking what all future cash that Trade Desk might generate is worth right now.

Trade Desk’s latest twelve month free cash flow is about $722.5 million. Using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, analysts and extrapolated estimates project free cash flow out over the next decade, with specific projections such as $917.8 million in 2026 and $1,751.2 million in 2030, all in $. Beyond the explicit analyst years, Simply Wall St extends the cash flow path using its own assumptions.

When those projected cash flows are discounted back, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $102.75 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $33.81, this implies the stock screens as around 67.1% undervalued based on this DCF output.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Trade Desk is undervalued by 67.1%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 884 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Trade Desk.

Approach 2: Trade Desk Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand because it links what you pay today directly to the earnings the business is already generating. It also reflects what the market is willing to pay for each dollar of profit, which is often where expectations and fears show up first.

A higher or lower P/E often comes down to two things: how much growth investors expect from earnings and how much risk they see in those expectations. Faster growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher uncertainty can line up with a lower one.

Trade Desk currently trades at about 37.28x earnings, compared with a Media industry average of about 14.40x and a peer group average of around 44.92x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary Fair Ratio for Trade Desk of 27.99x. This Fair Ratio aims to capture what a reasonable P/E might look like for this specific company by incorporating its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margin, market cap and risk factors, rather than relying only on broad peer or sector comparisons.

On that basis, Trade Desk’s current P/E of 37.28x sits above the Fair Ratio of 27.99x, suggesting the shares screen as overvalued on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1425 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Trade Desk Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, a simple way for you to put a clear story behind the numbers you are using for fair value, future revenue, earnings and margins.

A Narrative is your view of how a company’s story might play out, linked directly to a financial forecast and then to a fair value, instead of treating the valuation as a black box.

On Simply Wall St, millions of investors use Narratives on the Community page as an accessible tool where you can set your own assumptions, see the implied fair value and compare it with the current share price to help decide whether the stock looks attractive or expensive to you.

Because Narratives update automatically as new information such as news or earnings is added to the platform, your view of Trade Desk can stay aligned with the latest data. One investor might build a Narrative that supports a high fair value based on optimistic revenue growth and margins, while another might use more cautious assumptions that lead to a much lower fair value estimate.

Do you think there's more to the story for Trade Desk? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Following recent decline, Visional, Inc.'s (TSE:4194) top shareholder CEO Soichiro Minami sees holdings value drop by 8.3%
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • Visional's significant insider ownership suggests inherent interests in company's expansion
  • The top 5 shareholders own 52% of the company
  • Institutional ownership in Visional is 30%

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

To get a sense of who is truly in control of Visional, Inc. (TSE:4194), it is important to understand the ownership structure of the business. The group holding the most number of shares in the company, around 37% to be precise, is individual insiders. That is, the group stands to benefit the most if the stock rises (or lose the most if there is a downturn).

And following last week's 8.3% decline in share price, insiders suffered the most losses.

In the chart below, we zoom in on the different ownership groups of Visional.

See our latest analysis for Visional

What Does The Institutional Ownership Tell Us About Visional?

Institutional investors commonly compare their own returns to the returns of a commonly followed index. So they generally do consider buying larger companies that are included in the relevant benchmark index.

We can see that Visional does have institutional investors; and they hold a good portion of the company's stock. This implies the analysts working for those institutions have looked at the stock and they like it. But just like anyone else, they could be wrong. It is not uncommon to see a big share price drop if two large institutional investors try to sell out of a stock at the same time. So it is worth checking the past earnings trajectory of Visional, (below). Of course, keep in mind that there are other factors to consider, too.

Visional is not owned by hedge funds. The company's CEO Soichiro Minami is the largest shareholder with 35% of shares outstanding. With 5.4% and 5.3% of the shares outstanding respectively, FMR LLC and Capital Research and Management Company are the second and third largest shareholders.

Our research also brought to light the fact that roughly 52% of the company is controlled by the top 5 shareholders suggesting that these owners wield significant influence on the business.

While studying institutional ownership for a company can add value to your research, it is also a good practice to research analyst recommendations to get a deeper understand of a stock's expected performance. There are plenty of analysts covering the stock, so it might be worth seeing what they are forecasting, too.

Insider Ownership Of Visional

The definition of company insiders can be subjective and does vary between jurisdictions. Our data reflects individual insiders, capturing board members at the very least. Company management run the business, but the CEO will answer to the board, even if he or she is a member of it.

Insider ownership is positive when it signals leadership are thinking like the true owners of the company. However, high insider ownership can also give immense power to a small group within the company. This can be negative in some circumstances.

It seems insiders own a significant proportion of Visional, Inc.. Insiders own JP¥128b worth of shares in the JP¥349b company. That's quite meaningful. It is good to see this level of investment. You can check here to see if those insiders have been buying recently.

General Public Ownership

The general public-- including retail investors -- own 33% stake in the company, and hence can't easily be ignored. This size of ownership, while considerable, may not be enough to change company policy if the decision is not in sync with other large shareholders.

Next Steps:

While it is well worth considering the different groups that own a company, there are other factors that are even more important.

I like to dive deeper into how a company has performed in the past. You can find historic revenue and earnings in this detailed graph.

Ultimately the future is most important. You can access this free report on analyst forecasts for the company.

NB: Figures in this article are calculated using data from the last twelve months, which refer to the 12-month period ending on the last date of the month the financial statement is dated. This may not be consistent with full year annual report figures.

data.title: Clorox Pursues GOJO Deal To Reshape Earnings Mix And Valuation Potential
data.description:
• Clorox (NYSE:CLX) has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire GOJO Industries, the maker of Purell.
  • The deal marks a major expansion of Clorox's presence in health and wellness and its professional hygiene offerings.
  • The transaction is framed as a transformational move for Clorox's health and hygiene segment.

Clorox, trading at $112.87 a share, is moving to reshape its business mix with the planned acquisition of GOJO. The company is highlighting this deal as important for its fastest growing and most profitable segment, which matters given that NYSE:CLX has seen mixed longer term returns, including a 37.4% decline over 5 years and a 27.9% decline over 1 year.

For you as an investor, the key question is how this transaction might influence Clorox's earnings profile, cost structure, and competitive footing in health and hygiene over time. Management is positioning the deal as a way to build scale and seek cost synergies, so the market will likely focus on execution, integration progress, and any updates on expected financial benefits as details emerge.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Clorox by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Clorox.

How Clorox stacks up against its biggest competitors

Quick Assessment

  • ✅ Price vs Analyst Target: At $112.87, Clorox trades about 6.7% below the US$120.41 analyst consensus target.
  • ✅ Simply Wall St Valuation: Simply Wall St currently views the shares as undervalued, with the stock trading 47.1% below its estimated fair value.
  • ✅ Recent Momentum: The 30 day return of 14.6% suggests the market is already reacting to the GOJO deal and recent updates.

Check out Simply Wall St's in depth valuation analysis for Clorox.

Key Considerations

  • 📊 The GOJO acquisition pushes Clorox further into health and wellness. This means more of your thesis is tied to this segment’s execution and margins.
  • 📊 Keep an eye on integration costs, any synergy targets management sets, and whether the P/E of 17.4 stays in line with the 17.2 industry average.
  • ⚠️ Clorox already carries a high level of debt and its 4.39% dividend is not well covered by free cash flows, which could limit financial flexibility if the deal is expensive.

data.title: NetEase Cloud Music Inc.'s (HKG:9899) Fundamentals Look Pretty Strong: Could The Market Be Wrong About The Stock?
data.description:
With its stock down 27% over the past three months, it is easy to disregard NetEase Cloud Music (HKG:9899). However, stock prices are usually driven by a company’s financial performance over the long term, which in this case looks quite promising. In this article, we decided to focus on NetEase Cloud Music's ROE.

Return on Equity or ROE is a test of how effectively a company is growing its value and managing investors’ money. In short, ROE shows the profit each dollar generates with respect to its shareholder investments.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

How To Calculate Return On Equity?

The formula for ROE is:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for NetEase Cloud Music is:

22% = CN¥2.6b ÷ CN¥12b (Based on the trailing twelve months to June 2025).

The 'return' is the yearly profit. That means that for every HK$1 worth of shareholders' equity, the company generated HK$0.22 in profit.

View our latest analysis for NetEase Cloud Music

What Has ROE Got To Do With Earnings Growth?

So far, we've learned that ROE is a measure of a company's profitability. Depending on how much of these profits the company reinvests or "retains", and how effectively it does so, we are then able to assess a company’s earnings growth potential. Generally speaking, other things being equal, firms with a high return on equity and profit retention, have a higher growth rate than firms that don’t share these attributes.

A Side By Side comparison of NetEase Cloud Music's Earnings Growth And 22% ROE

To start with, NetEase Cloud Music's ROE looks acceptable. On comparing with the average industry ROE of 9.2% the company's ROE looks pretty remarkable. This probably laid the ground for NetEase Cloud Music's significant 82% net income growth seen over the past five years. However, there could also be other causes behind this growth. For instance, the company has a low payout ratio or is being managed efficiently.

Next, on comparing with the industry net income growth, we found that NetEase Cloud Music's growth is quite high when compared to the industry average growth of 25% in the same period, which is great to see.

Earnings growth is an important metric to consider when valuing a stock. What investors need to determine next is if the expected earnings growth, or the lack of it, is already built into the share price. This then helps them determine if the stock is placed for a bright or bleak future. Has the market priced in the future outlook for 9899? You can find out in our latest intrinsic value infographic research report.

Is NetEase Cloud Music Efficiently Re-investing Its Profits?

NetEase Cloud Music doesn't pay any regular dividends to its shareholders, meaning that the company has been reinvesting all of its profits into the business. This is likely what's driving the high earnings growth number discussed above.

Summary

Overall, we are quite pleased with NetEase Cloud Music's performance. Particularly, we like that the company is reinvesting heavily into its business, and at a high rate of return. Unsurprisingly, this has led to an impressive earnings growth. With that said, the latest industry analyst forecasts reveal that the company's earnings growth is expected to slow down. To know more about the latest analysts predictions for the company, check out this visualization of analyst forecasts for the company.

data.description:

The ROSELLA news has arrived after a volatile stretch, with a 31.4% 7 day share price return lifting the stock to US$45.64. This comes even though the 30 day share price return is a 44.8% decline, the 1 year total shareholder return is a 24.3% loss, and the 3 year total shareholder return is up 98.5%. This suggests long term holders have still seen strong gains despite recent swings.

If this ovarian cancer update has you watching the sector more closely, it could be a useful moment to scan other healthcare stocks that might be reacting to similar clinical or regulatory milestones.

data.title: Is ASML Holding (NasdaqGS:ASML) Pricing In Too Much Optimism After Its 1-Year Surge?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether ASML Holding's share price still makes sense after its strong run, this article focuses squarely on what you are paying versus what you might be getting.
  • The stock last closed at US$1,413.35, with returns of 4.0% over 7 days, 31.8% over 30 days, 21.4% year to date, 106.5% over 1 year, 122.5% over 3 years and 164.9% over 5 years. This performance has put valuation firmly in the spotlight for many investors.
  • Recent commentary around ASML has centered on its role in the semiconductor supply chain and how investors are thinking about long term demand for chipmaking equipment. These themes help frame why the share price has moved the way it has and why valuation is now such a key talking point.
  • ASML currently has a valuation score of 1/6, based on how many of six checks suggest the shares look undervalued. Next we will look at what different valuation approaches are saying and then finish with a way to judge whether those numbers truly fit your own view of the business.

ASML Holding scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: ASML Holding Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today’s value, so you can compare that estimate with the current share price.

For ASML Holding, the model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. It is based on cash flows in € rather than the US$ share price. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about €8.56b. Analysts have provided forecasts for several years, and Simply Wall St extrapolates further ahead, with projected free cash flow of €20.08b in 2030. The ten year path in the model runs from around €8.80b in 2026 up to €29.83b in 2035, with each year discounted back to today.

Putting all those discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of about US$916.26 per share, compared with the recent share price of US$1,413.35. On this basis, the DCF implies the shares trade at roughly a 54.3% premium to the modelled value, so the stock screens as overvalued on this measure.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests ASML Holding may be overvalued by 54.3%. Discover 884 undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for ASML Holding.

Approach 2: ASML Holding Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like ASML Holding, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each unit of current earnings. It helps you compare the share price to the underlying profit, which is often the starting point for many investors.

A “normal” or “fair” P/E tends to be higher when investors expect stronger earnings growth and lower business risk, and lower when growth expectations are modest or risk is higher. With ASML, the current P/E is 48.07x. That sits above the Semiconductor industry average of 40.81x and below the peer group average of 53.54x, so the stock is priced at a premium to the wider industry but not to the highest end of its peer set.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for ASML is 33.58x. This is a proprietary estimate of what the P/E might look like after factoring in earnings growth, profitability, risk profile, industry and market cap, rather than just lining it up against simple peer or industry averages. Because it is built around ASML’s specific characteristics, it aims to be a more tailored yardstick than broad comparisons. On this basis, the current P/E of 48.07x sits well above the Fair Ratio of 33.58x, which points to the shares looking expensive on this measure.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1425 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your ASML Holding Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives. These let you attach a clear story to your numbers by linking your view of ASML Holding’s business to assumptions about future revenue, earnings and margins, and then to an estimated fair value.

On Simply Wall St, within the Community page used by millions of investors, a Narrative is your personal, easy to set up framework that connects what you believe about the company to a financial forecast, and then to a fair value that you can directly compare with today’s share price.

That comparison, Fair Value versus current Price, can help you decide whether you see ASML Holding as a potential opportunity, something to hold, or an investment you might want to avoid. Your Narrative will keep updating automatically as new information such as news, guidance or earnings is added to the platform.

For ASML Holding, one investor might build a Narrative with a relatively high fair value that reflects strong expectations for future demand for chipmaking equipment, while another might use much more cautious revenue and margin assumptions that produce a much lower fair value. Both investors can clearly see how their story, numbers and decisions connect.

Do you think there's more to the story for ASML Holding? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: New York Tipping Ruling Tests Uber Eats Economics And Investor Expectations
data.description:
• A federal judge has rejected Uber and DoorDash's attempt to block New York City's new tipping law for food delivery apps.
  • The rule requires platforms to present a tipping option at checkout for customers ordering through these apps.
  • The law is set to take effect in New York City, affecting the way Uber Eats and rival services handle tipping flows.
  • Delivery platforms now face new compliance requirements and potential shifts in customer behavior and courier earnings.

For Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER), this ruling affects a core part of its business: the Uber Eats delivery segment in one of its largest urban markets. Food delivery has become a key pillar alongside the ride hailing operation, and New York City is an important test bed for regulatory ideas that can influence other regions. For investors, this adds another data point to how gig-economy rules are evolving for large app based platforms.

Looking ahead, you may want to watch how Uber adjusts app design, tipping prompts, and courier pay structures in response to the law. The way customers respond to the mandated tip screen, and how earnings for delivery workers are affected, could inform future policy debates and set reference points for other cities considering similar rules.

data.title: Equinox Gold Refocuses On North America With Record Output And Upside Potential
data.description:
• Equinox Gold (TSX:EQX) has completed the sale of its Brazilian mines Aurizona, RDM, and the Bahia Complex to CMOC Group.
  • The company is using the proceeds to reduce debt and sharpen its focus on North American gold operations.
  • Equinox Gold has restructured a gold offtake agreement with Vox Royalty, shifting deliveries from Brazilian assets to the Greenstone mine in Ontario.
  • The company has reported record annual gold production and highlighted contributions from the Greenstone and Valentine projects.

For investors watching gold miners, Equinox Gold (TSX:EQX) is now more concentrated in Canada and the United States, with Brazil no longer part of its mine portfolio. The company remains a pure gold producer, which can appeal to investors who prefer direct exposure to the metal rather than diversified commodity exposure.

The key assets to monitor are Greenstone in Ontario and Valentine, which the company expects to contribute to production. The combination of a refocused asset base, a revised royalty offtake and record annual production places greater attention on how efficiently Equinox Gold can operate and expand its North American operations.

data.title: WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings Ltd (HKG:9985) Not Flying Under The Radar
data.description:
WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings Ltd's (HKG:9985) price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 23x might make it look like a strong sell right now compared to the market in Hong Kong, where around half of the companies have P/E ratios below 12x and even P/E's below 7x are quite common. However, the P/E might be quite high for a reason and it requires further investigation to determine if it's justified.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Recent times have been advantageous for WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings as its earnings have been rising faster than most other companies. It seems that many are expecting the strong earnings performance to persist, which has raised the P/E. You'd really hope so, otherwise you're paying a pretty hefty price for no particular reason.

Check out our latest analysis for WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings

Keen to find out how analysts think WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings' future stacks up against the industry? In that case, our free report is a great place to start.
Advertisement

How Is WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings' Growth Trending?

There's an inherent assumption that a company should far outperform the market for P/E ratios like WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings' to be considered reasonable.

If we review the last year of earnings growth, the company posted a worthy increase of 11%. Pleasingly, EPS has also lifted 378% in aggregate from three years ago, partly thanks to the last 12 months of growth. Accordingly, shareholders would have probably welcomed those medium-term rates of earnings growth.

Shifting to the future, estimates from the twelve analysts covering the company suggest earnings should grow by 16% per annum over the next three years. That's shaping up to be materially higher than the 14% each year growth forecast for the broader market.

With this information, we can see why WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings is trading at such a high P/E compared to the market. Apparently shareholders aren't keen to offload something that is potentially eyeing a more prosperous future.

The Bottom Line On WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings' P/E

Generally, our preference is to limit the use of the price-to-earnings ratio to establishing what the market thinks about the overall health of a company.

As we suspected, our examination of WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings' analyst forecasts revealed that its superior earnings outlook is contributing to its high P/E. At this stage investors feel the potential for a deterioration in earnings isn't great enough to justify a lower P/E ratio. It's hard to see the share price falling strongly in the near future under these circumstances.

You always need to take note of risks, for example - WEILONG Delicious Global Holdings has 1 warning sign we think you should be aware of.

If P/E ratios interest you, you may wish to see this free collection of other companies with strong earnings growth and low P/E ratios.

data.title: How Investors May Respond To RingCentral (RNG) AI Voice Pivot And New AT&T Partnership
data.description:
• In recent months, RingCentral has repositioned itself as an AI voice company, rolling out new AI-driven communications tools for small and mid-sized businesses and extending its reach through partnerships such as AT&T.
  • An interesting angle is that these AI products both expand RingCentral’s estimated market opportunity by about US$65 billion and partially replace its older offerings, reshaping how the company generates growth.
  • We’ll now examine how RingCentral’s AI voice focus and new AT&T partnership influence the company’s broader investment narrative.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 109 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is RingCentral's Investment Narrative?

To own RingCentral today, you have to believe its shift from legacy unified communications toward AI voice can meaningfully refresh a business that is growing revenue in the mid‑single digits and only recently returned to profitability. The new AI Receptionist and Conversation Expert tools, plus the expanded AT&T partnership, speak directly to that thesis: they open a claimed US$65 billion incremental market and could give near‑term revenue catalysts a much‑needed boost if adoption among small and mid‑sized businesses gains traction. At the same time, some of this AI growth replaces older products rather than stacking on top, so the key question is whether higher‑value AI workloads offset that cannibalisation and support better margins. With a new AI‑focused director on the board and heavy buybacks, the story now leans even more on execution, balance sheet repair and AI product uptake than earlier analyses suggested.

However, investors should also weigh how negative equity and weak interest coverage could limit RingCentral’s flexibility. RingCentral's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Four Simply Wall St Community fair values span roughly US$33 to above US$100, showing just how far apart individual views on RingCentral run. Set that against the company’s heavy buybacks, recent AI repositioning and reliance on new products to offset cannibalisation, and it becomes clear you are choosing between very different interpretations of the same numbers.

data.title: Does WEC’s Leadership Transition and Higher Dividend Payout Reshape Its Long‑Term Capital Allocation Story (WEC)?
data.description:
• WEC Energy Group has announced that long-time chairman Gale Klappa will retire from the board after the May 2026 annual meeting, with current president and CEO Scott Lauber expected to assume the additional role of chairman, and has approved a quarterly dividend increase to US$0.9525 per share, lifting the annual rate to US$3.81.
  • In a first for the company’s more than 125-year history, Klappa will receive the honorary title of Chairman Emeritus, underscoring his influence at WEC and across Milwaukee’s business community through his ongoing economic development work.
  • With WEC Energy Group’s recent board leadership transition plan and higher dividend, we’ll explore how these developments shape its investment narrative.

We've found 13 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

What Is WEC Energy Group's Investment Narrative?

To own WEC Energy Group, you really have to believe in a steady, regulated utility that leans on consistent earnings, a long dividend record and disciplined capital allocation rather than big surprises. The board’s plan for a smooth handover from long-time chair Gale Klappa to current CEO Scott Lauber fits that story: it signals continuity in strategy and culture more than change, so it is unlikely to alter the near term earnings or dividend outlook in a material way. The higher US$3.81 annual dividend reinforces that income remains central to the thesis, even as free cash flow coverage and interest costs stay on the watchlist. Short term, the more immediate catalysts still look like upcoming earnings releases and any updates on funding plans after the US$3.00 billion equity shelf.

However, investors should be aware of how rising funding costs and weak cash coverage could pressure flexibility. WEC Energy Group's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

data.title: Sartorius’ Expanded ISCC Plus Network Could Be A Game Changer For Sartorius (XTRA:SRT3)
data.description:
• Sartorius AG recently expanded its bio-circular product offering, securing ISCC Plus certification for additional production sites in Germany and Tunisia to use renewable raw materials in PFAS-free filters and single-use fluid management technologies.
  • This move deepens Sartorius’ alignment with sustainability-focused bioprocessing by cutting fossil-based plastic use while keeping its equipment compatible with existing biopharma workflows.
  • Next, we’ll examine how Sartorius’ broader ISCC Plus-certified production network and reduced fossil-plastic reliance influence its overall investment narrative.

What Is Sartorius' Investment Narrative?

The big picture for Sartorius is still about owning a specialist supplier to biopharma that is trying to repair credibility after a tough few years, while justifying a very rich earnings multiple and meaningful leverage. The expanded ISCC Plus-certified network deepens its sustainability credentials and helps future proof key single use products, but it is unlikely to move the needle on near term results compared with core drivers like order momentum and margin recovery when the next earnings dates land in early and mid 2026. Where it may matter is on the risk side: tighter PFAS regulation and customer ESG requirements are front and center for bioprocessing, and Sartorius’ push into PFAS free, bio circular materials slightly lowers regulatory and reputational risk, which could support its premium pricing if execution holds up.

However, investors should also recognize how Sartorius’ high valuation multiple amplifies these execution risks. Sartorius' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 24%. Find out what it's worth.

data.title: How Investors Are Reacting To Nexans (ENXTPA:NEX) Leadership Shake-Up And Boardroom Change
data.description:
• Nexans recently overhauled its Executive Committee, installing 13 executives in newly defined roles across legal, strategy, HR/ESG, regional leadership, and key business lines to streamline operations and reinforce group-wide execution.
  • In parallel, director José Francisco Pérez Mackenna resigned from the board and its main committees, after being appointed Chile’s Minister of Foreign Affairs, highlighting Nexans’ governance links to international public policy circles.
  • We’ll now examine how this broad Executive Committee reshaping influences Nexans’ investment narrative, particularly around operational efficiency and governance.

Uncover the next big thing with financially sound penny stocks that balance risk and reward.

Advertisement

What Is Nexans' Investment Narrative?

For Nexans, the big-picture belief is that a focused electrification player with improving profitability and a still-modest earnings multiple can keep translating execution into shareholder value, even if revenue and earnings are expected to slip over the next few years. The recent Executive Committee overhaul under a relatively new CEO reinforces that this is now very much a “new management story,” with clearer regional accountability and dedicated leadership for high-value activities like data centers and grid projects. In the short term, the main catalysts still sit around the upcoming FY2025 results and how management talks about acquisition plans, margins and cash use, and the executive reshuffle is unlikely to change those near-term drivers in a material way. Where it may matter more is on the risk side, by adding execution and integration risk on top of already high leverage and earnings volatility, especially with another board departure and a new CFO only just stepping in.

However, Nexans’ elevated debt and fresh management team introduce extra execution risk investors should understand. Nexans' shares are on the way up, but they could be overextended by 31%. Uncover the fair value now.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Seven fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly €95 to €160, underlining how far apart private investors can be on Nexans. Set against the management shake-up and forecast earnings declines, these diverging views give you a useful cross-check before deciding how much weight to put on the current earnings multiple and recent share price strength.

data.title: Subdued Growth No Barrier To ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Company Limited's (NSE:ICICIPRULI) Price
data.description:
ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Company Limited's (NSE:ICICIPRULI) price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 67.5x might make it look like a strong sell right now compared to the market in India, where around half of the companies have P/E ratios below 23x and even P/E's below 13x are quite common. Although, it's not wise to just take the P/E at face value as there may be an explanation why it's so lofty.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Recent times have been advantageous for ICICI Prudential Life Insurance as its earnings have been rising faster than most other companies. It seems that many are expecting the strong earnings performance to persist, which has raised the P/E. If not, then existing shareholders might be a little nervous about the viability of the share price.

See our latest analysis for ICICI Prudential Life Insurance

Want the full picture on analyst estimates for the company? Then our free report on ICICI Prudential Life Insurance will help you uncover what's on the horizon.
Advertisement

What Are Growth Metrics Telling Us About The High P/E?

In order to justify its P/E ratio, ICICI Prudential Life Insurance would need to produce outstanding growth well in excess of the market.

Retrospectively, the last year delivered an exceptional 40% gain to the company's bottom line. The latest three year period has also seen an excellent 78% overall rise in EPS, aided by its short-term performance. Therefore, it's fair to say the earnings growth recently has been superb for the company.

Looking ahead now, EPS is anticipated to climb by 20% each year during the coming three years according to the analysts following the company. That's shaping up to be similar to the 20% each year growth forecast for the broader market.

In light of this, it's curious that ICICI Prudential Life Insurance's P/E sits above the majority of other companies. It seems most investors are ignoring the fairly average growth expectations and are willing to pay up for exposure to the stock. These shareholders may be setting themselves up for disappointment if the P/E falls to levels more in line with the growth outlook.

What We Can Learn From ICICI Prudential Life Insurance's P/E?

It's argued the price-to-earnings ratio is an inferior measure of value within certain industries, but it can be a powerful business sentiment indicator.

We've established that ICICI Prudential Life Insurance currently trades on a higher than expected P/E since its forecast growth is only in line with the wider market. Right now we are uncomfortable with the relatively high share price as the predicted future earnings aren't likely to support such positive sentiment for long. Unless these conditions improve, it's challenging to accept these prices as being reasonable.

The company's balance sheet is another key area for risk analysis. Take a look at our free balance sheet analysis for ICICI Prudential Life Insurance with six simple checks on some of these key factors.

You might be able to find a better investment than ICICI Prudential Life Insurance. If you want a selection of possible candidates, check out this free list of interesting companies that trade on a low P/E (but have proven they can grow earnings).
